factors.summary: Demand is accelerating in several segments.

evidence.text: Cost of sales increased $254 million, 10%, and $380 million, 7%, in the second quarter and first half of 2016, respectively, compared with the corresponding periods of 2015, largely due to higher volume at the Textron Systems, Textron Aviation, and Industrial segments, and an increase from acquired businesses.
evidence.subsection: Cost of Sales and Selling and Administrative Expense

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand acceleration in several segments indicates positive growth.

factors.summary: Order backlog is mixed with some segments seeing decreases while others grow.

evidence.text: Bells backlog decreased $316 million during 2016, as total deliveries, largely under the V-22 program, exceeded new orders. Textron Systems revenues increased $165 million, 51%, in the second quarter of 2016 compared with the second quarter of 2015, primarily due to higher volume of $103 million in the Weapons and Sensors product line and higher volume of $37 million in the Unmanned Systems product line.
evidence.subsection: Cost of Sales and Selling and Administrative Expense

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed backlog signals with some segments decreasing and others growing.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments.

evidence.text: The portion of the segments revenues derived from aftermarket sales and services increased to 34% of its total revenues in the second quarter of 2016, from 31% in the second quarter of 2015, in part, due to the impact from the acquisition.
evidence.subsection: Cost of Sales and Selling and Administrative Expense

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shift towards higher-margin segments suggests positive margin development.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating in several segments.

evidence.text: Revenues at Textron Systems increased $165 million, 51%, in the second quarter of 2016 compared with the second quarter of 2015, primarily due to higher volume of $103 million in the Weapons and Sensors product line and higher volume of $37 million in the Unmanned Systems product line.
evidence.subsection: Cost of Sales and Selling and Administrative Expense

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Acceleration in several segments shows strong organic growth.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing across multiple segments.

evidence.text: In the second quarter of 2016, Textron Aviations revenues increased $72 million, 6%, compared with the second quarter of 2015, primarily due to higher volume and mix of $52 million and the impact from the acquisition of a repair and overhaul business in the first quarter of 2016.
evidence.subsection: Cost of Sales and Selling and Administrative Expense

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volume growth across segments indicates positive demand.

factors.summary: End markets are growing fastest in the Textron Systems and Industrial segments.

evidence.text: Revenues at Textron Systems increased $165 million, 51%, in the second quarter of 2016 compared with the second quarter of 2015, primarily due to higher volume of $103 million in the Weapons and Sensors product line and higher volume of $37 million in the Unmanned Systems product line.
evidence.subsection: Cost of Sales and Selling and Administrative Expense

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong growth in specific segments suggests positive momentum.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable with decreases in share-based compensation.

evidence.text: The decrease in selling and administrative expense in the first half of 2016 was largely due to lower share-based compensation expense.
evidence.subsection: Cost of Sales and Selling and Administrative Expense

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Decrease in share-based compensation indicates cost management.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are improving in some segments.

evidence.text: Textron Systems segment profit increased $39 million, 186%, and $40 million, 82%, in the second quarter and first half of 2016, respectively, compared with the corresponding periods of 2015, largely reflecting higher volume as described above and a favorable product mix in 2016.
evidence.subsection: Cost of Sales and Selling and Administrative Expense

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving margins in some segments show significant positive development.
